question: Iran closes its airspace by May 31st?
reason: Iran has imposed temporary airspace closures and restrictions amid heightened tensions with the U.S.
evidence: On May 22, 2026, Iran's Civil Aviation Organization announced the temporary closure of airspace over western Iran, suspending all civil flight permits in the western sector of Tehran's Flight Information Region.
On May 23, 2026, Iran's Civil Aviation Organization announced temporary restrictions on the western part of the country's airspace.
On May 22, 2026, Iran ordered western airspace closed to non-day flights until Monday, adding to earlier emergency restrictions amid an acute standoff with the United States.

On May 22, 2026, Iran issued NOTAMs closing the western part of its airspace to night-time flights until Monday and state media warned a possible third round of fighting with the U.S. would feature new equipment, tactics, and trans-regional fronts beyond the Middle East.

On May 22, 2026, Iran has closed western portions of its airspace to non-daytime flights until Monday, formalized via NOTAMs. In parallel, military sources warn of a more advanced 'third phase' response if the US or allies take hostile action.
